input.initialPrompt: Analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this project. I haven't run any coverage yet.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for their project. They haven't run any coverage yet, so I need to first understand the project structure, then set up and run tests with coverage, and finally analyze the results.

Let me start by exploring the project structure.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-f43b96ab1d5f439c8601572592f406b4 && dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" --results-directory ./TestResults --verbosity quiet 2>&1
arguments.description: Run tests with code coverage collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with code coverage collection

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the project. Let me run the tests with code coverage using coverlet and then analyze the results.

The project already has `coverlet.collector` as a dependency. I can run `dotnet test` with coverage collection enabled, then analyze the results.

Let me run the tests with coverage.
